rulings.Case Title: KESHA CROMARTIE VS. NORTHRIDGE COOPERATIVE HOMES,INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion; Petitioners Motion For Judgment On Petition For Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 11, 2023 line 1. PETITIONER'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE is DENIED. This petition was filed on June 17, 2023 seeking specific relief outlined therein. The relief sought in this motion for judgment encompasses issues not before the Court on the Petition as currently drafted. Due to the passage of time and the events that subsequently transpired (termination of subsidy which Petitioner seeks to reinstate by the motion), the relief requested may be futile (2021 recertification) or moot (dissemination of private information in conjunction with recertification process). Petitioner may file an amended petition and seek appropriate relief. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: RONALD TANEGA VS. RUSSELL TANEGA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 11, 2023 line 3. DEFENDANT RUSSEL TANEGA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is DENIED. Moving party failed to carry its burden. There is no order deeming admission admitted on file. The order on file, a copy of which is attached to the Request for Judicial Notice orders the Plaintiff to provide complete verified responses to Request for Admissions within 5 days of notice of entry of order. This order tracks the Court's uncontested tentative ruling and is prepared by defendant's counsel. (See July 17, 2023 minutes). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ANSON KWAN VS. MING YOU FENG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 11, 2023 line 4. DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden and show its entitlement to a judgment as a matter of law. Moving party failed to show that Plaintiff had to comply with SFRO 37.9(o). Points and Authorities are devoid of any discussion, analysis or even reference to the last sentence of this provision. Moving party failed to cite any authority for the proposition that waiver applies to cases under CCP 1161(4) and SFRO 37.9(a)(3). The only cases cited arise out of breach of lease. No analysis/argument of applicability of these authorities to the facts of this case provided. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: UMI HOSPITALITY VS. CHALEDEEANNKA GOYENS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: APPLICATION TO PREVENT FORFEITURE DUE TO COVID-19 RENTAL DEBT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 11, 2023 line 7. DEFENDANT ROBERT EBERWEIN'S APPLICATION TO PREVENT FORFEITURE DUE TO COVID-19 RENTAL DEBT is DENIED. Rent demanded in the operative Notice is for January to June 2023, not a period covered by UD-125/CCP 1179.13 application. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
